headline: FOURTH MAJOR FIND: Americas Gold Hits 1,392 g/t Silver at Idaho's Galena Complex
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) announced its fourth major new discovery at its Galena Complex in Idaho, defining six new silver-copper-antimony veins in the 43L-TJ Vein Complex — including 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu and 1.5% Sb over 1.9m — located just 25m from existing mine infrastructure.

headline: RECORD QUARTER: Global Gold Demand Hits $193 Billion as China Hoards 207 Tonnes
snippet: The WGC's Q1 2026 Gold Demand Trends report shows total demand hit a record value of US$193bn (+74% YoY), driven by a surge in investment demand. Chinese bar and coin buying rose 67% to an all-time quarterly record of 207 tonnes as retail investors piled in amid geopolitical uncertainty.

headline: SIX STRAIGHT YEARS: Silver Deficit Widens 15% to 46.3 Million Ounces in 2026
snippet: The silver market confirmed its sixth consecutive annual supply deficit in 2026, with the shortfall widening 15% to 46.3 million troy ounces. Above-ground inventories have been drawn down by 762 million ounces since 2021 — roughly a full year of global mine output — with no supply response yet sufficient to close the gap.

headline: Glencore Q1 Copper Output SURGES 19%; War-Driven Sulphur Costs Threaten H2 Margins
snippet: Glencore reported own-sourced copper of 199,600 tonnes in Q1 2026, up 19% year-on-year on improved African grades and higher Antamina throughput. The company held 2026 guidance but CEO Gary Nagle flagged rising diesel and sulphur costs from the Gulf conflict as an emerging pressure heading into H2.

headline: BATTERY METALS SHOCK: Huayou Halts Half Its Indonesia Nickel Plant as Sulphur Hits $800/t
snippet: Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt will shut half the production lines at its Huafei Nickel Cobalt HPAL plant in Indonesia from May 1 after spot sulphur prices surged past $800/t due to Gulf supply disruptions from the Iran conflict. The cut threatens battery-grade nickel supply just as EV and energy-storage demand accelerates.

headline: Emperor Metals Builds to 1.46Moz at Duquesne West; MONSTER HIT 21.7m at 35.2 g/t Au
snippet: Emperor Metals (CSE: AUOZ) reported an updated inferred resource of 26.9Mt at 1.69 g/t Au (1.46Moz) at its Duquesne West project in Quebec, with a standout intercept of 21.7m at 35.2 g/t Au highlighting high-grade expansion potential. The company is 11,030m into a 23,000m campaign targeting an open-pit development path.

headline: Traction Uranium Maps 2026 Drill Campaign at Athabasca Basin's Aurora Project
snippet: Traction Uranium (CSE: TRAC) outlined a two-phase 2026 exploration program at its Aurora Project near Key Lake, Saskatchewan — starting with an airborne radiometric survey followed by diamond drilling to test shallow uranium targets. Aurora spans 17km of prospective strike on the southeastern Athabasca margin, 16km from the past-producing Key Lake mill.

headline: WORLD BANK WARNING: Gold and Silver Near Cyclical Peak; 7% Price Retreat Forecast for 2027
snippet: The World Bank's April Commodity Markets Outlook forecasts gold averaging ~$4,700/oz and silver ~$70/oz in 2026, signalling a cyclical peak near current levels. The bank projects a ~7% price decline in 2027 as speculative demand fades, central bank buying slows, and war-related risk premiums ease.

headline: THE CASE FOR $6,000 GOLD: De-Dollarization, War Risk, and a Broken Rate Cycle
snippet: A GoldSeek analysis argues that structural tailwinds — U.S.-Iran tensions, accelerating central bank reserve diversification, and the Fed's stalled rate-cut cycle — create a credible path to $6,000/oz gold by end-2026. The piece notes mining equities remain historically discounted against bullion, suggesting leveraged upside for producers.

headline: Mining M&A Hits $21.6 BILLION in Q1 2026 — Strongest Start to a Year Since 2023
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026, up 34% in value year-on-year, according to White & Case data. Critical-minerals supply-chain security dominated deal rationale, with strategic partnerships displacing outright acquisitions as buyers target stable-jurisdiction assets for the energy transition.

headline: RARE EARTH MOMENTUM: Supply-Chain Security Drive Lifts Western REE Stocks
snippet: Rare earth equities are gaining momentum as Western governments accelerate supply-chain de-risking from China. USA Rare Earth's $2.8B Serra Verde acquisition and Aclara Resources' Carina feasibility study are cited as catalysts reshaping the ex-China REE supply landscape ahead of U.S. Strategic Reserve stockpiling under Project Vault.
